key_insight: 3D-PRIMME learns a local, physics-regulated evolution rule for grain growth that preserves coarsening laws and topology across vastly different 3D scales.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, scalable surrogate simulation of 3D microstructure evolution for materials design and process optimization without retraining on larger domains.
method: Trained on only two consecutive time steps to learn grain-growth dynamics.
Uses physics regulation to enforce grain-boundary-energy reduction and statistical invariants.
Applies the learned operator to much larger 3D domains without retraining.
evidence_signal: The model was trained on a 100^3 grid with 512 grains and extrapolated to 1024^3 grids with 550,000 grains while maintaining linear coarsening and topological statistics.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the physics constraints and training data capture the target material system, especially for conditions outside the learned regime.
paper_title: A Physics-Regulated Neural Framework for Learning 3D Grain Growth Dynamics
